steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize calcite's Mohs scale number (3). The correct answer must make that specific number the primary focus of the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Calcite has a Mohs scale number of 3, which means that it is harder than talc (1) but softer than orthoclase (6).’: leads with the number directly and immediately explains what it means, making 3 the central fact. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘In the Mohs scale of mineral hardness, orthoclase (6) is ranked higher than talc (1).’: buries the number or frames calcite primarily in terms of another property, so the Mohs number is not the main emphasis. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Orthoclase, which has a Mohs scale number of 6, can scratch both talc and calcite.’: mentions calcite in a context where the Mohs number is secondary to another point. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Calcite, talc, and orthoclase can be ordered by their ability to leave visible scratches on other minerals.’: similarly fails to foreground the specific Mohs scale number of 3 as the primary claim. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
